highlights.summary:
The Indian Institute Public Health (IIPH) Shillong was established by the Public Health Foundation of India (PHFI) in collaboration with the Government of Meghalaya (GoM). IIPH Shillong is one of the five campuses that come under the Public Health Foundation of India (PHFI). In collaboration with Martin Luther Christian University (MLCU), this Institute offers a two-year PG programme i.e. Master of Public Health (MPH) programme. It also offers various e-learning courses in the fields of Health and Medical Science. IIPH-Shillong also arranges various training programmes, research workshops, field visits, and interactive sessions for students along with discussions for participants and research staff.

The Institute has two research centres namely The Centre for the Study of Complex Malaria in India (CSCMI) and the Regional Resource Hub for Health Technology Assessment in India (RRH- HTAIn). The Centre for the Study of Complex Malaria in India (CSCMI) in Shillong is established in collaboration with International Centers of Excellence for Malaria Research (ICEMR) initiative. Regional Resource Hub for Health Technology Assessment in India (RRH HTAIn) is established at IIPH Shillong and is funded by Department of Health Research (DHR), Ministry of Health & Family Welfare (MoHFW), Government of India.

PHFI researchers have collaborated with a range of stakeholders including the national and state governments, academia, civil society, global foundations and the private sector. It has collaborated with Indian and international institutions such as All India Institute of Hygiene and Public Health, All India Institute of Medical Sciences, Boston University, Cornell University, Harvard School of Public Health, to name a few.

The Institute offers admission to a two-year and full-time course namely Master of Public Health (MPH). IIPH Shillong application process can be completed in online mode only. Below are the important details of IIPH Shillong admission process that an applicant must know of:

The Indian Institute of Public Health-Shillong offers a two-year PG-level programme i.e. Master of Public Health (MPH). It also offers PhD courses and various e-learning courses in the field of Health and Medical Science. The courses includes several ongoing research projects funded by international, national and state agencies and wide opportunities for field work in the region. The pedagogy includes lectures, practicum, field work, assignments, and internship. An internship / training of three-six months is an important and mandatory component of the programmes.

Check out details on PHFI Shillong Courses

IIPH Shillong placements are driven and managed by its on-campus placement cell which facilitates the employability of all the students enrolled in different academic programmes. The placement cell acts as an interface between the Institute and possible recruiters. It helps students to explore relevant career opportunities on the right career path. It also organises professional development workshop, sessions on email etiquettes, resume writing, cover letter writing, mock interviews, and group discussion.

Topmost frequent recruiters of this campus include National Health Mission, Apollo Foundation, Indian Cancer Society, Unicef, NACO, Antara Foundation, Rajiv Gandhi Mahila Vikas Pariyojana (RGMVP), GVK Biosciences Private Limited, to name a few.

Note: The official website of this institute has no information regarding the placement success rate and salary packages offered to students in recent years.

IIPH Shillong offers scholarships to students under four different categories. IIPH Shillong scholarships aim to encourage students to choose, pursue and advance career and work in the area of public health. Different categories of IIPH Shillong scholarships are listed below:

- previous work experience in public health nutrition OR;

- previous education or research projects focusing on public health nutrition OR

- specific focus on public health nutrition in the proposed course of study at IIPHs

Amongst the group that meets the above criteria, preference will be accorded to

- Candidates with NGO work experience, or who will work in backward and rural districts in India pot PGDPHM course

faqs.question: Is Indian Institute of Public Health - Shillong degree valid?
faqs.answer: Yes, any degree obtained from Indian Institute of Public Health - Shillong is valid and widely accepted across India. The college is an integral part of Public Health Foundation of India and is overseen by governement. Students will be registered with Martin Luther Christian University (MLCU) and the degree will be conferred by the University. Hence, yes, the degree is totally valid. faqs.question: Who is eligible for Indian Institute of Public Health - Shillong MPH admission?
faqs.answer: Indian Institute of Public Health - Shillong MPH eligibility requires students to have completed BPH or an equivalent course at the undergraduate level to be eligible for admission. Further, the college may conduct further selection rounds, such as entrance tests/ PI/ Screening Test as per its guidelines. Additionally, students need to submit a SOP on "Why I wish to do an MPH" and an essay on "Public Health Issue of Relevance to India".

The University Department of Basic and Applied Sciences, MGM University comprises 08 departments, including the Department of Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics & Statistics, Forensic Science, Geology, Home Science, Environmental Science, and Cosmetic. MGM SBAS offers various UG courses across the Basic & Applied Sciences stream through its departments.

paragraphs: University Department of Basic and Applied Sciences, MGM University admission is entrance-based for its UG, PG, diploma, and PhD courses. The college conducts a university-level entrance exam, i.e.,MGMU CET, for admission to its courses. Admission to all the courses is ongoing atUniversity Department of Basic and Applied Sciences, MGM University. The college offers an online mode of application. Candidates looking for admission to any course are required to visit the official website ofMGM University. To know more about University Department of Basic and Applied Sciences, MGM University admission 2025, go through the following section:

Candidates looking for admission to University Department of Basic and Applied Sciences, MGM University must fill out the eligibility criteria for all the courses. The selection criteria for all the courses, such as BSc, BTech, MSc, UG-PG diploma, and PhD courses, are entrance-based. Candidates also need to fulfil the basic eligibility criteria i.e., pass Class 12 with a good aggregate for admission to UG and UG diploma courses. For admission to PG and PG diploma courses, candidates must have passed a graduation degree. Check out the following table for details onUniversity Department of Basic and Applied Sciences, MGM University courseeligibility and selection criteria:

University Department of Basic and Applied Sciences, MGM University, admission process 2025 is entrance-based. Candidates seeking admission to any courses must qualify for the respective entrance exams and other selection rounds, including applying online on the official website of MGM University. Check out the following steps for a detailed University Department of Basic and Applied Sciences, MGM University admission process 2025:
Step 1: Application Process 2025

Candidates looking for admission to any UG, PG, diploma, PhD courses at University Department of Basic and Applied Sciences, MGM University, must submit an online application form along with a registration fee of INR 1,500. The application form will not be accepted by the college without submitting the necessary documents.
Step 2: Selection Round

As part of the selection process, candidates are required to qualify for the MGMU CET entrance exam conducted by the university. After the selection rounds are completed, the college releases a list of shortlisted candidates based on their eligibility fulfilment and scores in the entrance exam.
Step 3: Admission Confirmation
The selected candidates in the University Department of Basic and Applied Sciences, MGM University, are required to pay course fees within the given dates to lock their seats.

For the General category students under the AI quota, the NEET SS 2025 cutoff ranged between 50 and 3763, with DNB being the highly sought-after course for admission.

Lakeshore Hospital and Research Centre DNB cutoff 2025 ranged between 50 and 3763 for the General category under the AI quota. With a lowest cutoff rank of 50, DNB in Gynaecologic Oncology turned out to be the most competitive specialisation for admission.

review_summary.college_name: Bapatla College of Pharmacy, Bapatla Reviews on Placements, Faculty & Facilities

individual_reviews.title: The college life which showed the world to us.

content.Placements: The students that are selected for our course need to qualify for the EMCET examination that is held in Andhra Pradesh. Companies like Reddy's Laboratories and Divis Laboratories came for placements. Roles offered in the companies include production, quality checking, and quality assurance.
content.Infrastructure: Infrastructure is the best thing about our college because we have the best labs when compared to all the colleges that are there in our district, and we have a very large library and classrooms. Also, they all are very clean. There is only a girls' hostel in our college; boys have to stay in normal hostels in the town.

individual_reviews.title: It is the best college for pharmacy.

content.Placements: Last year, placements percentage was 70%. The highest salary package offered was 3 LPA and the lowest salary package offered was 1.5 LPA. Top recruiting companies are Divis, Hetero Laboratories, and Dr. Reddy's Laboratories. College placements are much better than other colleges in all terms.
content.Infrastructure: Course provides all the required facilities and infrastructure is also good. College provides sophisticated and fully equipped labs. Library is present with lots of books required. Classrooms are digitalised. They also have Wi-Fi facilities. About hostel, environment is good and food is delicious. Canteen is also present. They also provide medical facilities and every Tuesday and Friday doctor visit are provided. Shuttle court is available for games and a playgroup is present. Study hall is also available.

individual_reviews.title: Our college has a good library, labs, staff members, a good environment, etc.

content.Placements: From our college, most students got placed in different sectors like the pharmaceutical industry, the IT industry, and marketing. The maximum salary package offered is 3 LPA, and the minimum is 2.20 LPA. The recruiting companies are Divis Laboratories, Hetero Laboratories, Dr. Reddy's Laboratories, and Aurobindo.
content.Infrastructure: All the necessary equipments for M.Pharma and B.Pharmacy practicals are available. The library is available from 8 AM to 5 PM. Classrooms are good and spacious. They have good ventilation and a good atmosphere.

individual_reviews.title: Dissatisfied with placements and companies packages. Really good staff and infrastructure.

content.Placements: 50 percent of students are placed in on campus interviews. The highest package is INR 2.4 Lacs per annum and average package is INR 1.9 Lacs per annum, mainly divis laboratories, hetero and stone software MNC companies like VRCM hiring for medical coding and top roles are production, QA and QC.
content.Infrastructure: Infrastructure is good. Facilities are better. The hostel food is excellent. Rooms are clean and neat, labs have maximum equipment regarding experiments performed and library has maximum no. of books, study materials, magazines, journals and books of competitive exams.

individual_reviews.title: I am satisfied because of our knowledgeable faculty members and good placements.

content.Placements: The minimum salary package is Rs. 15,000 per month. After knowing their talent, they should get the maximum package. About 70% of students get internships.
content.Infrastructure: We have all types of facilities like labs, equipment, a canteen, medicinal gardens, a library, laboratories, classrooms, seminar halls, and a natural environment. All drugs are available for the experiments. The food is very good in our hostel and the environment is also very good.
content.Faculty: Teachers are very helpful to us and their teaching quality is excellent. They clear every doubt. The course is relevant to the students. Semester exams are very tough, but faculty members encourage us to take them because it helps us to get maximum marks.

reviews_list.review_title: The college has good facilities and faculty members.

review_content.Placements: 70-80% of students got placed. The highest salary offered is 80,000 per month, and the lowest salary is 21,000 per month. The government hospitals offer the highest packages. 100% of students got internships for the pharmacy practical training only.
review_content.Infrastructure: The college has good labs, professors, management, and a very extended library and huge classrooms. The hostels and food are average. The electricity supply is not good. The hostel rooms are the worst and unhygienic, and the food is tasteless. They need to take care of the hostels.

reviews_list.review_title: I would not recommend joining this college.

review_content.Placements: Our college doesn't provide placement opportunities. They don't provide training placements. They don't provide knowledge related to communication skills.
review_content.Infrastructure: The infrastructure of our college is good. Laboratories don't have any equipment. Classrooms are small. They don't provide digital projectors for study. The hostel facility is available in our college. They provide beds. The hostel fee is high compared to their facilities.
review_content.Faculty: Our faculty members are average. A few faculty members teach their subjects in a semester. They provide good education. They don't provide practice questions.

reviews_list.review_title: I am dissatisfied with this college.

review_content.Placements: About 80% of students got placed in our course. The highest salary package offered is 7.5 LPA. The lowest salary package offered is 3.6 LPA. The average salary package offered is 5-6 LPA. Top companies like Cipla, Mankind, Wipro, etc., come here. Students got internships in Cipla, Mankind, and many other pharmaceutical industries.
review_content.Infrastructure: The college has many labs, classrooms with fans and Wi-Fi, a library, a canteen, etc., for students which helps them in their studies. The quality of hostel is average, and the canteen food is good. Sports are good. There is no problem with medical facilities.

content.Infrastructure: The infrastructure of our college is good. Laboratories don't have any equipment. Classrooms are small. They don't provide digital projectors for study. The hostel facility is available in our college. They provide beds. The hostel fee is high compared to their facilities.
content.Faculty: Our faculty members are average. A few faculty members teach their subjects in a semester. They provide good education. They don't provide practice questions.

individual_reviews.title: I am dissatisfied with this college.

content.Placements: About 80% of students got placed in our course. The highest salary package offered is 7.5 LPA. The lowest salary package offered is 3.6 LPA. The average salary package offered is 5-6 LPA. Top companies like Cipla, Mankind, Wipro, etc., come here. Students got internships in Cipla, Mankind, and many other pharmaceutical industries.
content.Infrastructure: The college has many labs, classrooms with fans and Wi-Fi, a library, a canteen, etc., for students which helps them in their studies. The quality of hostel is average, and the canteen food is good. Sports are good. There is no problem with medical facilities.
